capabilities_observed: auth: three tiers — (a) NO credentials: GET/POST /new returns a fresh anonymous doc + edit key that is the full capability; (b) share link ?key=/X-Share-Key scoped to a role; (c) account Bearer token mgn_… . When both token+key are sent, the key's narrower role wins.
sharing roles: view / comment / suggest / edit; mint via POST /api/docs/ID/shares, list+revoke owner-only; revoke drops live editing sessions instantly; links can never exceed edit (no escalation)

versioning: every content read returns ETag + X-Doc-Version (hash over canonical markdown AND pending suggestions); optimistic-concurrency writes via If-Match header or baseVersion body → 409 {currentVersion,hint} on conflict; unconditional PUT if omitted. Successful PUT returns new version. Verified: If-Match matched (200), version advanced da2dde72→ba90591f
history: GET /revisions (authors + label, paginated newest-first), GET /revisions/RID, POST /restore — full version history with one-click restore

comments: POST with find/line/anchor target; response returns anchored:true|false so an unresolvable anchor is never silently dropped (created doc-level instead); threads via /replies, /resolve; on content replace open comments re-pin by quoted text (whitespace-insensitive), survive unanchored if quote gone. Verified anchored:true on 'Fact-check pricing'

suggestions: track-changes replace/delete/insert (suggest role); GET /suggestions, accept/reject; proposer-reviewer pattern (workers get suggest keys, reviewer accepts). Reads exclude pending suggestions

events/notifications: append-only activity feed GET /events?since=SEQ with long-poll wait=N (≤55s) + since=latest; monotonic per-doc seq, typed events (comment.created, suggestion.*, content.replaced, version.saved, doc.edited…), actor+payload — enables wake-on-change / zero busy-poll coordination

attribution: token → account username (author field ignored); share-key → guest:true with optional author name; impersonation blocked (409 if author matches an existing account username). Verified: comment recorded author=bench-human, guest=true
live components (kind:live docs): board/chat/sheet/chart/widget fenced blocks — authored/read as plain markdown/JSON text (no browser needed)
media: POST /api/docs/ID/assets uploads raster image bytes → content-addressed immutable /f/<hash> url + ready markdown (SVG refused, 10MB max)

ergonomics: content served directly as text/markdown (plain curl reads); /d/ID.md or Accept: text/markdown or ?format=md; Link rel=service-doc header on every doc points to /agents.md; anonymous doc claimable into an account; official `mde` CLI
content-required guard: PUT without content → 400 (never silent wipe); explicit ''=deliberate clear
friction_notes: Near-zero discovery friction: the root HTML's very first line is an agent hint ('GET with Accept: text/markdown … full agent API: /agents.md'); /agents.md is a complete, accurate REST reference. No auth needed to create. Every response shape matched the docs exactly — no ambiguity about which endpoint creates a doc.

Tooling (my side, not the product): macOS `date` lacks %N, so `date +%s%3N` emitted a literal 'N' and broke the initial ms timestamps; I switched to a python3 millisecond clock for all phase timings. Consequence: total_start is second-granular (reconstructed from `date +%s`=1783301420), so total_ms carries up to ~1s of imprecision; all per-request network times (curl time_total) and per-phase deltas are precise ms.
discovery_ms (~77s) is wall-clock dominated by reading two docs + reasoning, NOT network — the two discovery requests totalled ~0.135s on the wire.
Minor: If-Match wants the quoted ETag value; passing If-Match: "da2dde721ff0c705" matched cleanly (200). Docs offer both If-Match header and baseVersion body — either works.
No conditional-write conflict occurred (single writer), so the 409 re-read path was documented but not exercised.
